content: Hi, I'm Rowan. I'm a Social Worker specializing in foster care and I'm looking to learn some new skills to help me with my work.
Hi Rowan! That's great to hear that you're looking to learn new skills to help with your work. What specific areas are you interested in learning more about?
I'm thinking maybe something related to counseling or therapy. I've been working with a lot of kids who have been through trauma and I want to be able to better support them.
That's a wonderful goal. There are many great resources available for learning about counseling and therapy. Have you considered taking any courses or attending workshops?
Yeah, I've been looking into that. There's a great course at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill that I'm thinking of taking. It starts on January 10th and I'm planning to attend.
That sounds like a great opportunity. What do you think you'll gain from taking this course, and how do you see it helping you in your work with foster care?

I'm hoping to gain a better understanding of how to support kids who have been through trauma, and to learn some new skills for counseling and therapy. I've also been talking to my colleague, Dr. Lee, who's an expert in the field and she's offered to mentor me.

Having a mentor can be really helpful when learning new skills. What do you think is the most challenging part of your work as a Social Worker, and how do you see this new skill helping you to overcome that challenge?

For me, it's probably just the emotional toll of the work. It can be really draining to work with kids who have been through so much. But I'm hoping that having some new skills and knowledge will help me to feel more confident and prepared to support them.

That makes sense. It's great that you're recognizing the importance of self-care and seeking out ways to support yourself in your work. Do you have any other hobbies or interests outside of work that help you to relax and recharge?
Yeah, I actually really enjoy playing the piano and traveling. I've been to some amazing places, like Japan and New Zealand, and I love experiencing different cultures.

That sounds wonderful! Traveling and experiencing different cultures can be such a great way to broaden your perspective and gain new insights. Have you ever considered incorporating your love of travel into your work, maybe by working with kids from different cultural backgrounds?
Actually, yeah! I've been thinking about that a lot lately. I'd love to be able to work with kids from different cultural backgrounds and help them to navigate the foster care system.

That's a great goal. It's always wonderful to see people using their passions and interests to make a positive impact in their work. Do you have any plans for how you'll make this goal a reality, or is that still in the works?

I'm thinking of starting by volunteering with a local organization that works with kids from different cultural backgrounds. And then maybe I'll look into taking some courses or attending workshops to learn more about cultural competency and how to support kids from different backgrounds.
That sounds like a great plan! Volunteering and taking courses are both great ways to learn and gain experience. I'm sure you'll make a positive impact in the lives of the kids you work with.
Thanks, I'm excited to get started! I'm also thinking of starting a travel blog to document my adventures and share them with others.

That sounds like a great idea! Starting a travel blog can be a fun way to share your experiences with others and reflect on your travels. Do you have any plans for your first blog post, or is that still in the works?
